title: rosa rubiginosa subsp. micrantha
intro: Rosa rubiginosa subsp. micrantha, also known as the dwarf sweetbriar or dwarf rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa rubiginosa plant, a deciduous shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Rosa rubiginosa subsp. micrantha include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce menstrual cramps, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional medicine to make salves and ointments for skin issues.
